This depends on the accuracy of Adhana lagna.
The seventh sutra may be explained in a different way.
The degrees progressed by the ascendant at birth should be
determined by multiplying the anuliptas by nine.
In every navamsa these are 720000 anuliptas. In each
sign there are 6480()()() anuliptas. If one degree of _navamsais
over, the ascendant at birth progresses by nine degrees in the
sign of the lagnas.
The eighth sutra asks us to take the navamsa of the lagna
at birth. From the navamsa of the birth chart, we have to
arrive at the chart of the Adhana lagna.
The Sutra only gives vague principles. At the most we can
arrive at the navamsa lagna which covers 200 liptas. Each
navamsa covers nearly thirteen to fifteen minutes.
4. Let us now turn to Varahamihira who suggested the
pre-natal epoch theory in one verse of his Brihajjataka.
